name: chr17_q21_31_MAPT_inversion_polymorphism
description: 17q21.31 H1/H2 inversion polymorphism — ~900 kb inversion spanning MAPT/CRHR1/KANSL1. H2 haplotype (~20% in Europeans, near-absent in Africans) is a primate-specific inversion under positive selection in Europeans. Inverted orientation creates direct-orientation SDs that mediate the recurrent 17q21.31 microdeletion (Koolen-de Vries syndrome) almost exclusively on H2 background.

architecture.internal_structure: Four major SD blocks (A-D) define inversion; H2 = inverted relative to ancestral H1; H1=80%, H2=20% Europeans, near-zero Africans

related_diseases: Koolen-de Vries syndrome (17q21.31 microdeletion) — exclusively on H2
